LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANDREAS AMUNDIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF HIS NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS NOT BEING CONTESTED, AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION AGAINST MCKESSOR: DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR, WHICH IS NORMALLY A QUESTION OF FACT. (SEE TENTATIVE RULING ON MCKESSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION AGAINST METHOD360: DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER BLACKSTONE CASE WAS BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S CONDUCT AS A METHOD360 EMPLOYEE. ISSUE RAISED WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS SUED BY BLACKSTONE BASED ON CONDUCT OCCURRING BEFORE METHOD360'S INVOLVEMENT. ALSO, HEARD AND ARGUED IS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: ATTORNEY'S FEES ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $6,885 TO BE PAID BY THE MCKESSON FIRM. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT METHOD360, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. ORIGINAL COMPLAINT FILED BY BTG AGAINST PLAINTIFF IMPLICATES DEFENDANT, ALLEGING UNFAIR COMPETITION AND THE TAKING AWAY OF BTG BUSINESS. THE COMPLAINT CONCERNS ACTIONS TAKEN BY PLAINTIFF WHILE EMPLOYED BY DEFENDANT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT METHOD360, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED FROM FEB-03-2010 TO FEB-11-2010, ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION MOTION PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 128.7 FOR AN ORDER STRIKING PLAINTIFF ANDREWS AMUNDIN'S COMPLAINT IN ITS ENTIRETY AND IMPOSING SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. NO SHOWING THAT CCP SECTION 128.7(B) HAS BEEN VIOLATED. ISSUE OF WHETHER AN INDIVIDUAL IS AN EMPLOYEE OR AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR IS A HIGHLY FACTUAL ISSUE. MERE DISCOVERY RESPONSES THAT SUGGEST ONE CATORIZATION OVER ANOTHER DO NOT NECESSARILY PROVE THAT CONTRADICTORY ALLEGATIONS PLEADED IN A SUBSEQUENT CASE WERE MADE FOR AN IMPROPER PURPOSE. ALSO, TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANT RELIES ON DISCOVERY RESPONSES IN THE BLACKSTONE LITIGATION, SANCTIONS MAY NOT BE AWARDED. CCP SECTION 128.7(G). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Set for hearing on Friday, April 9, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORP. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Leila Narrid, Esq. & Chuck Louderback, Esq. of Payne & Fears LLP (415) 398-7860 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jennifer G. Redmond, of Sheppard Mullin (415) 434-9100, appearing for Defendant McKesson and moving party; Eli W. Gould, Esq. of MBV Law Group, (415) 781-4400 appearing for Defendant Methold360. (See minutes in Word document for the Court's ruling, there is not enough space to enter the ruling in mini-minutes) (302) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ANDREAS AMUNDIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION of his ninth cause of action for declaratory relief. Appearances: Leila Narrid, Esq. & Chuck Louderback, Esq. of Payne & Fears LLP (415) 398-7860 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Jennifer G. Redmond, of Sheppard Mullin (415) 434-9100, appearing for Defendant McKesson; Eli W. Gould, Esq. of MBV Law Group, (415) 781-4400 appearing for Defendant Methold360. Plaintiff's motion is not being contested, and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Motion against McKessor: denied. Triable issues of fact whether plaintiff was an employee or independent contractor, which is normally a question of fact. (See tentative ruling on McKesson's motion for Summary Judgment. Motion against Method360: denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether Blackstone case was based on plaintiff's conduct as a Method360 employee. Issue raised whether plaintiff was sued by Blackstone based on conduct occurring before Method360's involvement. Also, heard and argued is Plaintiff's motion for attorney's fees. The Court rules as follows: Attorney's fees are awarded to Plaintiff's counsel in the amount of $6,885 to be paid by the Mckesson Firm. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT METHOD360, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Charles Louderback, Esq. of Payne & Fears LLP (415) 398-7860 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Eli W. Gould, Esq. MBV Law LLP (415) 781-4400 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Original complaint filed by BTG against plaintiff implicates defendant, alleging unfair competition and the taking away of BTG business. The complaint concerns actions taken by plaintiff while employed by defendant. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn For Sanctions; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Appendix Of Foreign Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION MOTION PURSUANT TO CCP Sec. 128.7 FOR AN ORDER STRIKING PLAINTIFF Andrews Amundin's Complaint in its entirety and imposing sanctions. Appearances: Leila Narvid, Esq. & Charles Louderback, Esq. of Payne & Fears LLP (415) 398-7860 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jennifer G. Redmond, Esq. of Sheppard Mullin (415) 434-9100 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. No showing that CCP section 128.7(b) has been violated. Issue of whether an individual is an employee or an independent contractor is a highly factual issue. Mere discovery responses that suggest one catorization over another do not necessarily prove that contradictory allegations pleaded in a subsequent case were made for an improper purpose. Also, to the extent defendant relies on discovery responses in the Blackstone litigation, sanctions may not be awarded. CCP section 128.7(g). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
